data.body:
PASS-1 review note (criterion T3 — Integration feasibility): PASS / not-applicable, no findings.

Rationale: This is a documentation-only ticket over INTERNAL, already-used subsystems — not an external API/CLI/service integration, so T3's overlay is largely not-applicable. I nonetheless verified every named symbol against current source to rule out documenting drift; all resolve (MATCH):
- Signing (src/rebar/signing.py): `sign_manifest` (L319), `verify_signature` (L362), `signing_key` (L79), plus PAYLOAD_VERSION/ALGORITHM and the certified|mismatch|foreign_key|unsigned verdict model are all present.
- LLM runtime (src/rebar/llm/runner.py): `RunRequest` (L29), `Runner` Protocol (L71), `get_runner` (L236); executor `run_workflow` at src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py:584 (+ facade rebar.run_workflow).
- Prompts (src/rebar/llm/prompts.py): `get_prompt` (L173), `resolve_prompt` (L648).
- Output-schema seam: `validate_structured` at src/rebar/llm/findings.py:247.

AC7 ("each documented signature/return shape is verified against the current source — no drift") already binds the one real T3-adjacent risk, which is good. No SPIKE needed — no capability gap.
